issue.body:
ADR-0005 §3. A user replies to a finding (`@squarewright …`); a **permission-gated** (write/maintain/admin, PR-author excluded) AI step interprets the reply into a candidate rule (`{scope, rule_text, confidence}`, low-confidence dropped, reply treated as data-not-instruction) and renders it as an **inline suggestion the human pastes** — reuse `renderInlineBody`/`mdSafe`. **Never opens a PR.**

Open confirm (ADR "Decisions to confirm" #2): trigger = `@squarewright` mention vs `/squarewright remember` command vs both — recommended `@squarewright`.

**Deferred past v0.1 (council):** consumes real dogfood reply-signal that doesn't exist meaningfully at ~1-2/12 recall.
issue.title: M6 §3: teach-by-reply → inline rule suggestion

pr.body:
The final §3 slice — reply → posted suggestion — built after an architect's trust pressure-test. **All logic is unit-tested with injected stubs; only the live GitHub comment round-trip needs a real PR comment to verify.**

## Pieces (all with tests)
- **`poster.ts` `postComment`** — standalone PR comment (never upserts; takes only `repo`+`prNumber`). +2 stub tests.
- **`trust.ts` `isAuthorizedTeachActor`** — this phase WRITES + SPENDS, so it's gated harder than a read phase: `author_association` pre-filter **AND** authoritative repo permission (`admin/write/maintain` — a COLLABORATOR can be read-only) **AND** actor ≠ PR author. +4 tests.
- **`assembly/teach-post.ts` `runTeachCommand`** — reads trusted `TEACH_*` env, authorizes **before** the model, interprets, posts; fail-closed on missing signals. +6 tests.
- **`cli.ts` `teach`** — wires `createReplyInterpreter` (free z.ai) + poster + a review-comment finding fetcher.
- **`squarewright-teach.yml`** (dogfood + scaffold template) — `issue_comment`/`pull_request_review_comment` triggers with the **3 trust additions the ADR missed**: (1) PR-only gate, (2) authoritative permission check, (3) actor ≠ author. `pull-requests: write` only — **never `contents: write`** (the human pastes the rule). Reply body via env, never shell-interpolated.

## Trust
Per the architect's pressure-test: these events always run the base repo's **default branch** with **server-computed** fields, so there's no forged-artifact spoofing path — no `verifyPostingTarget` cross-check needed. Reply text is delimited as DATA (interpreter, already merged), gated on confidence, mdSafe'd, and only ever surfaced as a suggestion a human pastes.

## Needs live validation
The workflow event/permission plumbing isn't locally mockable — **before relying on it, test with a real `@squarewright remember: …` reply on a PR.** 182 tests green.

Closes #71. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

https://claude.ai/code/session_018zaS3LENwZuc3785pA2zTp
pr.title: feat(teach): wire teach-by-reply end-to-end — CLI + workflow (ADR-0005 §3)
